macro_events.event_summary: Trump announced he is postponing a "scheduled attack of Iran" at the request of Middle East leaders (Saudi/UAE/Qatar), signaling near-term de-escalation of US-Iran military confrontation.
primary_drivers: Geopolitical risk premium compression in oil
Lower probability of Strait of Hormuz disruption

beneficiaries.rationale: Jet fuel is ~20-25% of operating costs; a pullback in crude on Iran de-escalation directly improves margins. Delta has higher fuel sensitivity than hedged peers.
Similar jet-fuel cost exposure as DAL; international routes through the Gulf region also benefit from reduced airspace/insurance risk.
Cruise line bunker fuel costs fall with crude; also direct beneficiary of reduced Middle East travel risk premium affecting Mediterranean/Red Sea itineraries.
Domestic carrier with heavy fuel cost leverage and limited hedging program in recent years; tracks crude moves tightly.

losers.rationale: Largest US integrated oil; upstream earnings directly tied to Brent. Removal of Iran strike premium compresses near-term crude price expectations.
High-beta US E&P with concentrated oil (not gas) exposure; among the most sensitive large-caps to crude price moves on geopolitical risk-off.
Lockheed makes munitions (JASSM, Hellfire, PAC-3) that would be expended/restocked in an Iran strike; postponement removes near-term order acceleration narrative.
Maker of Tomahawk, SM-3, Patriot interceptors — direct beneficiaries of Middle East kinetic action. Defense names typically give back gains on de-escalation headlines.
Oilfield services revenue tied to upstream capex, which tracks crude prices; lower oil = weaker activity outlook.

headlines: Trump says he's postponing 'scheduled attack of Iran tomorrow' at Middle East leaders' request

macro_events.event_summary: Kevin Warsh will be sworn in as Fed Chair at the White House, the first such ceremony in nearly 40 years, signaling unusually close alignment between the new Fed leadership and the executive branch.
primary_drivers: Perceived erosion of Fed independence / politicization risk premium
Warsh's known hawkish-on-balance-sheet but dovish-on-growth lean and history of criticizing QE
Likely steeper yield curve and weaker USD on independence concerns

beneficiaries.rationale: Newmont is the largest US-listed gold miner with high operating leverage to the gold price; a sustained move in spot gold on Fed-independence concerns translates roughly 2-3x into NEM earnings.

headlines: Kevin Warsh will be the first Fed chair sworn in at the White House in almost 40 years
